calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-20-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker for Brenda Fennell.

Mr. Boessenekcer stated that he received no further opposition. The Court approved the Petition for Final Distirbution (signing the order for the same in open court).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Sandberg.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-19-2011 Continued To Apr-20-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-30-2011 Continued To Apr-19-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker for and with Brenda Fennell (Petitioner).

Mr. Boessenecker stated that he just filed acknowledgements of receipt of both petitions from all 3 children, addressed the seperation issue, stated that notice was given to his Client's Mother in Law, that his Client was raising a child from another relationship and that he has an order showing his Client as legal Guardian. The Court stated that it would be helpful to have a declaration from Ms. Fennell regarding the seperation issue of the Parties. The matter was continued to April 19, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-16-2011 Continued To Mar-30-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Off Calendar Oct-25-2010 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar May-26-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker for and with Brenda Fennell (Petitioner). Carolyn Baulsier for and with Doris Fennell (Petitioner).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to their Client's priority to serve and Probate Code 8468 (failure to Claim Appointment). Mr. Boessenecker explained the reason for using a mailing address instead of a street address, and stated that he had nominations from all three Children and that he would file them. Ms. Baulsier stated that the insurance company indicated that no Beneficiary was listed on the policy. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
